name: Sy Oliver
description: American jazz arranger, trumpeter, composer, singer and bandleader (1910–1988)
abstract: Melvin James "Sy" Oliver (December 17, 1910 – May 28, 1988) was an American jazz arranger, trumpeter, composer, singer and bandleader.

concerts.label: The New York Jazz Repertory Company: Duke Ellington 75th Birthday Salute
Newport Jazz Festival: Music of Tommy Dorsey and McKinney's Cotton Pickers

works.t: On the Sunny Side of the Street

works_created.t: T'ain't What You Do (It's the Way That You Do It)

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Jones
about_quotes.evidence: Sy Oliver is mentioned by the interviewer in a list of musicians and arrangers (Paul Whiteman, Benny Goodman, Fletcher Henderson, Count Basie, Tommy Dorsey) without any statement from the interviewee indicating a personal or professional connection.

about_quotes.speaker: Al Grey
about_quotes.evidence: I ran into Sy Oliver and he had the band at that time at the Empire State Building... So he signed me on to play in the studio band
about_quotes.doc_title: Al Grey interviewed by Michael Woods, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 3, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Buck Clayton
about_quotes.evidence: I was out there with Sy Oliver and Mercer... And we had ten trumpets in Camp Kilmer, including Sy and myself.

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Waters
about_quotes.evidence: But the bands that I think were great were Jimmie Lunceford... and Sy Oliver, you know. They all did well.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Waters interviewed by Michael Woods, Los Angeles, California, September 1, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Barefield
about_quotes.evidence: And Sy Oliver was there in the band. ... And we were stationed in Columbus, Ohio. And Sy Oliver was there

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: I told him that I played in Sy Oliver's orchestra before I came to France
